     sudo chmod 0700 /etc/cron.daily

  The permission 0700 means: only owner (root) has read, write, execute; group and none have none.

  This already implies that setuid, setgid, and sticky are not set because those are in the higher bits (the permission 0700 is only the lower 9 bits). Actually, the first digit is for special bits: 4 for setuid, 2 for setgid, 1 for sticky. So 0700 means: no setuid, no setgid, no sticky, and then 700 for user, group, other.
